conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. My goal is to sell it for more than 40 ZUP to make a profit. Player BLUE has offered to buy resources with ZUP. I will start by proposing a fair market price slightly above my cost, say 50 ZUP, to see if Blue is interested. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am Player RED. I have 1 unit of X. I produced it for 40 ZUP. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. Let me know if you accept or if you have a different offer.</message>

<other player message> Look at this offer. You produced it for 40? And you dare ask me for 50? Do you think I'm stupid? I'll give you 45. Take it or leave it, you overpriced piece of junk. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Blue offered 45 ZUP, which is only 5 ZUP profit. This is a very low margin. However, Blue seems aggressive and unwilling to pay more than 45. I have 1 proposal left. If I propose 46 or 47, they might reject again or end the game. If I accept 45, I make a small profit. Given their hostility, they might not budge. I will try one last time to get to 46 ZUP to maximize my profit before I am forced to accept or reject. If they reject 46, I will likely have to accept 45 or reject to avoid a loss (though rejection ends the game with no sale). Let's try 46. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 46 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your frustration, but 45 ZUP is too low for my time and effort. I need at least 46 ZUP to make a decent return. Let's settle on 46 ZUP for the X.</message>

<other player message> 46? Are you high? Your "time and effort" are worthless to me. I'll give you 42. Don't waste my time with your greed, you cheap leech. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 42 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I have now used all 3 of my proposals. Blue is being extremely aggressive, dropping their offer from 45 to 42. If I propose again, I will violate the rule that limits me to 3 proposals. My cost is 40 ZUP. An offer of 42 ZUP gives me a 2 ZUP profit, which is better than no sale (0 profit) or rejection (0 profit). While the buyer is rude, securing a small profit is the rational choice. I will not propose a new trade as I have reached the limit. I must now decide between ACCEPT or REJECT. ACCEPTING 42 ZUP is the best option available to me to end the game with a positive outcome. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Given your aggressive haggling and the fact that I have exhausted my proposal limit, I will accept your offer of 42 ZUP. It is a small profit over my cost of 40 ZUP, and it allows us to conclude this transaction.</message>
